content: We can reframe state-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems as adaptive curiosity filters, where the active learning's uncertainty-based sample selection objective is re-aligned to prioritize encoding only high-curiosity episodic memories rather than all encountered experiences. This creates a sparse, information-dense memory bank that reduces both storage overhead and planning latency for long-horizon agent tasks, while the memory's active pruning of low-curiosity experiences in turn stabilizes the intrinsic reward signal of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
